action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 11 achieved only 0.7432 AUC, well below the best 0.7594 from iteration 6. The LSTM-DKT architecture seems sound but recent runs consistently underperform. Analysis: A likely root cause is that the LSTM processes zero-padded timesteps as real interactions (skill 0, incorrect) because our embedding mapping never truly masks padding in the recurrence. By using pack_padded_sequence with the actual sequence lengths, the LSTM will ignore padded positions entirely, eliminating hidden-state contamination. This principled fix should recover (and potentially exceed) the 0.7594 peak. Decision: Update model.py to accept lengths and use pack_padded_sequence/ pad_packed_sequence with proper sorting/unsorting. Update train.py and predict.py to pass lengths, with backward compatibility for old checkpoints. Train with the proven config (embed=256, hidden=256, layers=2, dropout=0.3) for up to 60 epochs and submit eval.
